steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The fellowship was created to publicly [verb] exceptional folk and traditional artists. The second sentence confirms the purpose is celebratory — Carmen Solis received it “to celebrate her lifetime contributions to the arts.” The blank requires a verb meaning to officially honor or acknowledge outstanding achievement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — startle: Means to alarm or surprise suddenly. Nothing in the text suggests artists were unprepared for or shocked by the fellowship; the tone is celebratory, not startling. ✗
  • Choice B — recognize: Means to officially acknowledge and honor someone for their achievements. Directly matches the fellowship’s stated purpose of celebrating lifetime contributions. ✓
  • Choice C — familiarize: Means to make something known or familiar to someone. The text describes recipients as “exceptional” — already distinguished — and gives no indication they were unknown before receiving the fellowship. ✗
  • Choice D — convey: Means to transport or communicate something. A fellowship does not transport artists, and saying it “conveys” the artists themselves is illogical. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
